summary:
During the nodular calcified stage of neurocysticercosis, there is a notable absence of edema because the cysts have ceased their pathologic activity leading to reduced immune response and inflammation. This contrasts with other stages where ongoing parasitic involvement results in persistent or increasing cerebral edema. To summarize:

The nodular calcified stage shows little to no edema since it marks an end-stage phase marked by complete cessation of parasite-induced pathology and associated inflammation; unlike more acute stages where active disease leads to sustained cerebrospinal fluid accumulation causing noticeable tissue swelling. 

Let me know if you would like me to modify anything!
